world.title: Turkey school shooting: 9 killed, many hurt; 2nd such incident in two days
world.summary: Nine people were killed and many others injured when a middle school student opened fire at a school in Kahramanmaras, southeastern Turkey, on Wednesday. The incident marks the second school shooting in the country within two days. Local authorities, including Governor Mukerrem Unluer, confirmed that a teacher was among the victims in this tragic event.

world.title: In Trump's Post On Strait Of Hormuz, A "China Very Happy" Message
world.summary: US President Donald Trump announced on Truth Social that he is "permanently opening" the Strait of Hormuz, stating that it will never be blocked again. He suggested that this decision makes China "very happy." Trump's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the strategic importance of the Strait, a vital route for global oil shipments and trade.

world.title: Turkey school shooting: 4 dead, 20 injured after gun attack at middle school
world.summary: A shooting at a middle school in Turkey resulted in four deaths, including three students and one teacher, and approximately 20 injuries. The incident prompted a swift response from security forces, who secured and cordoned off the area. Reports indicate that the attacker was an eighth-grade student. The situation is ongoing, with investigations currently underway.

business.title: India slips to sixth-largest economy in 2025, now seen becoming third-biggest by 2031
business.summary: India is projected to become the sixth-largest economy in 2025, according to the International Monetary Fund, dropping one rank from the previous year. This decline is attributed to a stronger dollar and GDP revisions, despite India maintaining one of the fastest growth rates among major economies. The country is expected to rise to the third-largest economy by 2031.

business.title: Snap to lay off 1,000 employees, CEO Evan Spiegel's memo puts it on AI; says: 'Rapid advancements in AI enable our teams to...'
business.summary: Snap is laying off 1,000 employees, representing 16% of its global workforce, as it shifts focus towards profitable growth and integrates artificial intelligence. CEO Evan Spiegel highlighted the rapid advancements in AI as a key factor in this decision, which aims to enhance efficiency and reduce costs. The layoffs reflect the company's strategic pivot in a challenging market.

business.title: HDB Financial Q4 results: Net profit rises 41% to Rs 751 crore, NBFC declares Rs 2 dividend
business.summary: HDB Financial Services reported a 41.4% increase in net profit for Q4 FY26, reaching Rs 751 crore, compared to Rs 531 crore in the same quarter last year. The company's interest income rose 13% to Rs 4,081 crore, up from Rs 3,623 crore. Additionally, the non-bank lender declared a dividend of Rs 2 per share.

technology.title: Scientists discover way to make Aluminium batteries affordable and safer, offering alternative to Lithium
technology.summary: Indian scientists have created a new composite electrode material for aluminium-ion batteries, enhancing stability and lifespan. This innovation addresses significant challenges in aluminium-ion technology, making these batteries more affordable and safer compared to traditional lithium-ion options. The development presents a promising alternative that is both eco-friendlier and potentially transformative for the battery industry, improving overall performance and longevity.

technology.title: 2026 KTM 1390 Super Duke RR aka The Beast Unveiled With 187 HP Engine
technology.summary: KTM has unveiled the 2026 1390 Super Duke RR, featuring the LC8 engine that delivers 187 horsepower. Positioned as the flagship model in its lineup, the motorcycle is designed for enhanced performance and agility. Dubbed "The Beast," it aims to offer a more focused riding experience, emphasizing its capabilities as a high-performance street machine.

technology.title: Oppo F33 and F33 Pro announced with 7,000mAh batteries and Dimensity 6360 Max chipsets
technology.summary: Oppo has announced the F33 and F33 Pro smartphones in India, featuring 7,000mAh batteries and Dimensity 6360 Max chipsets. Both models are available for pre-order, with deliveries set for April 26. The F33 mirrors the global A6 Pro, while the F33 Pro closely resembles the Chinese A6 series. The devices maintain familiar designs and specifications.

sports.title: Rs 25.20 Crore Star Cameron Green Now A 'Liability', KKR Told To Drop Australian By India Great
sports.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) have secured only one point after five matches in IPL 2026, with four losses and one washed-out game against Punjab Kings. Former Indian cricketer has labeled Australian player Cameron Green, who was acquired for Rs 25.20 crore, as a "liability" and advised KKR to consider dropping him from the team.

sports.title: IPL 2026: Match 24, MI vs PBKS Match Prediction - Who will win today’s IPL match between Mumbai Indians vs Punjab Kings?
sports.summary: Mumbai Indians (MI) face Punjab Kings (PBKS) in Match 24 of IPL 2026 at Wankhede Stadium on April 16. MI has struggled in their campaign this season, while PBKS aims to capitalize on their performance. Fans and analysts are eager to see the outcome of this high-stakes match, with predictions circulating about which team will secure victory.

sports.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Set To Break Sachin Tendulkar's Record. Who's Youngest Cricketer To Make International Debut?
sports.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, a 15-year-old left-handed batter from Bihar, is poised to break Sachin Tendulkar's record as the youngest player to debut for the India men’s cricket team. He has been included in the list of probable players for the upcoming T20I series against Ireland, marking a significant milestone in his budding cricket career.

health.title: HIV outbreak horror: 331 children infected in Pakistan's Punjab, unsafe injection practices found at govt hospital
health.summary: At least 331 children in Punjab, Pakistan, have contracted HIV between November 2024 and October 2025, linked to unsafe injection practices at a government hospital. An investigation revealed that among 97 affected children, only four had HIV-positive mothers, suggesting mother-to-child transmission was not the primary cause. The outbreak was first noticed by a local doctor in late 2024.

health.title: VAR Checks take on new meaning as Bally Bet Pledges donations per check to boost testicular cancer awareness
health.summary: Bally Bet has announced a donation of £100 to The OddBalls Foundation for each VAR check during the Premier League matches this weekend. This initiative is part of their "Check Your Bally’s" campaign, aimed at raising awareness for Testicular Cancer Awareness Month. The campaign seeks to give a meaningful purpose to VAR checks while promoting health awareness.

health.title: PharmEasy promotes preventive heart care in campaign
health.summary: PharmEasy has launched a World Health Day campaign promoting preventive heart care through real-life storytelling. The campaign features Prashant Desai, who shares his personal experience of losing family members, including his father at 37, to heart disease. The initiative aims to raise awareness and encourage heart health screenings, emphasizing the importance of preventive measures in heart care.

science.title: Scientists Say the Universe Could Eventually Tear Itself Apart Under This Extreme Scenario
science.summary: A new scientific model indicates that the universe may eventually tear itself apart due to accelerated expansion, potentially leading to a scenario known as the Big Rip. This phenomenon could be influenced by quantum effects, suggesting that the cohesive fabric of spacetime might not remain intact. The research highlights the complex dynamics of cosmic evolution since the Big Bang.

science.title: Sperm whale communication shows striking similarities to human language: study
science.summary: A new study reveals that sperm whales exhibit vocal communication patterns similar to human language. Researchers discovered that the whales use an "alphabet" of clicks, called codas, which can create vowel-like sounds by varying length and tone. This finding underscores the complexity of sperm whale social interactions and suggests advanced communication abilities among these marine mammals.

science.title: How Hidden Undercurrents Are Reshaping Long-Term Dynamics
science.summary: Himalayan forests are experiencing a hidden regeneration crisis, with key species such as deodar, chir pine, and banj oak struggling to establish seedlings and saplings. Factors like dense monocultures, altered fire regimes, and invasive plants are disrupting forest structure. This threatens long-term ecosystem stability, despite the appearance of stable forest cover, highlighting the importance of continuous regeneration.

entertainment.title: Rs. 112 Crore Just for a Title? What LIK Makers Did Next Will Surprise You
entertainment.summary: "Love Insurance Kompany" (LIK), featuring Pradeep Ranganathan and Krithi Shetty, was released five days ago but has performed poorly at the box office. The film, a futuristic romantic drama, has not met expectations, raising questions about its title's cost of Rs. 112 crore. The production team is now considering strategies to improve its reception and overall performance.

entertainment.title: Bhooth Bangla Box Office Day 1 Advance Booking (2 Days To Go): Akshay Kumar Starrer Sells 10K+ Tickets Across Country!
entertainment.summary: Bhooth Bangla, featuring Akshay Kumar, is set to release on April 17, with paid previews on April 16 at 9 pm. The film has sold over 10,000 tickets in advance bookings across India. Despite this initial success, the film needs to improve its advance sales to achieve a strong box office performance following the success of Dhurandhar 2.

entertainment.title: EXCLUSIVE: No direct-to-OTT release for Maatrubhumi; Salman Khan determined to bring his film to cinemas
entertainment.summary: Salman Khan's film Maatrubhumi will not have a direct-to-OTT release, as he is committed to bringing it to cinemas. Additionally, following directives from the Ministry of Defence, the film will not mention China. This decision has generated significant discussion within the Bollywood community, highlighting the film's anticipated release and the impact of governmental guidelines on its content.
